concept.name: Virtual Memory
concept.paragraph_1: Virtual memory is a memory management technique that allows processes to execute even if they are not completely loaded into physical memory.
concept.paragraph_2: It relies on paging or segmentation to divide memory into manageable units.
concept.paragraph_3: This abstraction improves memory utilization and provides isolation between processes.

concept.name: CPU Scheduling
concept.paragraph_1: CPU scheduling is the mechanism by which an operating system selects which process in the ready queue should be executed next.
concept.paragraph_2: Scheduling algorithms aim to optimize performance metrics such as turnaround time, waiting time, throughput, and CPU utilization.
concept.paragraph_3: Common scheduling strategies include First-Come First-Served, Shortest Job First, Priority Scheduling, and Round Robin.

concept.name: CPU Scheduling
concept.paragraph_1: CPU scheduling is the mechanism by which an operating system selects which process in the ready queue should be executed next.
concept.paragraph_2: Scheduling algorithms aim to optimize performance metrics such as turnaround time, waiting time, throughput, and CPU utilization.
concept.paragraph_3: Common scheduling strategies include First-Come First-Served, Shortest Job First, Priority Scheduling, and Round Robin.

concept.paragraph_2: It relies on paging or segmentation to divide memory into manageable units.
concept.paragraph_3: This abstraction improves memory utilization and provides isolation between processes.

concept.paragraph_2: Scheduling algorithms aim to optimize performance metrics such as turnaround time, waiting time, throughput, and CPU utilization.
concept.paragraph_3: Common scheduling strategies include First-Come First-Served, Shortest Job First, Priority Scheduling, and Round Robin.
